channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Unveiling the molecular mechanism of Qingwen Baidu decoction against dengue fever: an integrated study of bioinformatic analysis, machine learning and network pharmacology
item.description: This study employed bioinformatic analysis, machine learning, and network pharmacology to elucidate the molecular mechanisms by which Qingwen Baidu decoction, a traditional herbal formulation, exerts activity against dengue fever. The integrated computational approach identified key molecular target

item.title: STAT+: A sweeping new AI to detect heart conditions is coming to OpenEvidence
item.description: EchoNext, a new AI tool cleared by the FDA, will let doctors use an EKG to easily screen patients for structural heart diseases.

item.title: Pre-market clinical investigation approval pathways and the role of early feasibility studies for medical devices: a scoping review to guide EU policy
item.description: This scoping review examines pre-market clinical investigation approval pathways for medical devices in the EU, with particular focus on the role and implementation of early feasibility studies to inform European regulatory policy development.

item.title: STAT+: Definium LSD therapy helped patients with major depression in late-stage trial
item.description: Definium Therapeutics said that its LSD therapy helped patients with major depression in its first Phase 3 trial, bringing the psychedelic drug closer to a potential approval.

item.title: Opinion: STAT+: Enough, already: the problem with clinical trial data collection
item.description: “Nearly 30% of the data collected in clinical trials do not directly inform key decisions, yet patients are still asked to provide it.”

item.title: Cw@60: on longevity – a career spanning 60 years of modern computing
item.description: On 22 September 2026, Computer Weekly turns 60. To mark the milestone, we asked some of our friends - experts, trusted contacts, IT leaders and suppliers - for their perspectives on how tech has changed their lives over six decades

item.title: The future of artificial intelligence in business operations and workplace efficiency
item.description: Artificial Intelligence in Business Operations is rapidly transforming how organizations function, compete, and innovate. From automating repetitive tasks to enabling data-driven decision-making, AI is reshaping the modern business landscape. As technology co…

item.title: Navigating the AI access control minefield
item.description: Rather like the early days of e-commerce, everyone seems to be ‘doing artificial intelligence’. IT leaders must now ensure these systems have secure access to enterprise data

item.title: Which data management costs should survive budget cuts?
item.description: Tighter budgets force hard choices about data governance. This breakdown of essential data management costs shows where to cut spending and where cuts create risk.

item.title: Malaysia to adopt a gradual approach to autonomous driving, to build regulatory, infra aspects first – nanta
item.description: Malaysia will take a gradual, safety-first approach to autonomous driving by building the necessary regulatory and infrastructure foundation for the technology before any large-scale deployment comes about. According to works minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nan…

item.title: Non-coding variant prioritization based on cell type, developmental stage and evolutionary constraint
item.description: By using deep learning sequence models, we predict non-coding variant effects across the allele frequency spectrum in over 100 fetal and adult cell types. Linking these data with evolutionary constraint helps prioritize variants across conditions, such as Alz…

item.title: Biological aging and generational shifts in early-onset cancer risk
item.description: Analyses of population cohorts found that young adults exhibited earlier systemic and organ-specific aging, which was associated with increased risk of early-onset cancer compared with older adults born decades earlier.

item.title: Collaborative care intervention for risky opioid use among primary care patients: The STOP randomized clinical trial
item.description: The STOP trial is a randomized clinical trial evaluating a collaborative care intervention designed to address risky opioid use in primary care patients. The intervention leverages a team-based approach involving primary care providers and addiction specialists to improve patient outcomes for opioid

item.title: Development of phenotype algorithms for the detection of adverse events in electronic health record data: a multicentre study
item.description: This multicentre study developed phenotype algorithms to automatically detect adverse events within electronic health record data. The research represents a collaborative effort to improve pharmacovigilance through systematic identification of drug-related safety signals in routine clinical data.

item.title: STAT+: Closely watched Pfizer lung cancer drug falls short in clinical trial
item.description: Pfizer said an experimental drug it hoped could replace a widely used chemotherapy in one of the most common forms of lung cancer fell short in a clinical trial.

item.title: STAT+: FDA to launch pilot program to speed up early-stage clinical trials
item.description: Federal health officials aim to speed up drug development and better compete with China.

item.title: STAT+: Another big deal, another sign biotech M&A is back
item.description: AbbVie buys Apogee Therapeutics, LSD therapy Phase 3 results, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: STAT+: Despite transplant referrals, most kidney patients don’t make the waitlist
item.description: An NIH grant proposal poses new threats, more cases of a livestock disease in Europe, and more health news from Morning Rounds

item.title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
item.description: Google DeepMind and A24 are teaming up to build AI filmmaking tools.

item.title: Three things to watch amid Anthropic’s latest feud with the government
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. For those of you enjoying your summer unaware of Anthropic’s latest feud with the US government, here’s a recap: In April the company said it had built an AI mode

item.title: How Anthropic may have talked itself into an AI export ban
item.description: The company warned about dangers of advanced AI far more than rival OpenAI.

item.title: Import AI 462: Superpersuasion; self-sustaining AI; paths to ASI
item.description: Welcome to Import AI, a newsletter about AI research. Import AI runs on arXiv, cappuccinos, and feedback from readers. If you’d like to support this, please subscribe. Subscribe now AI can decisively out-persuade humans:…“AI systems were reliably more persuasive than expert humans”…Researchers

item.title: How openai and anthropic ipos could affect your retirement savings
item.description: OpenAI and Anthropic both anticipate going public at valuations approaching $1 trillion, yet they remain unprofitable and are spending billions of dollars a year on computer processing.

item.title: J. d. vance’s AI doctrine
item.description: The vice president is making a case that is part Silicon Valley, part MAGA.

item.title: AI in marketing: your guide to legal compliance, data sovereignty, and regulatory risk management
item.description: Legal Disclaimer: This article provides general information and commentary on legal matters for educational purposes only. TrinityP3 is not a legal practitioner and does not provide legal advice. The content contained herein should not be relied upon as a sub…

item.title: Africa’s data dilemma: lessons from building an offline AI model for health (randomforest) and agriculture (mobilenetv3)
item.description: When I began my MSc Artificial Intelligence dissertation, I thought the most challenging part would be designing a dual‑domain model that could serve both Health and Agriculture and still run offline on low‑end devices with as little as 2GB RAM. I was wrong.

item.title: Accelerating drug discovery with mass spectrometry-driven bioanalytical innovations and beyond
item.description: This article reviews mass spectrometry-driven bioanalytical innovations that support drug discovery, particularly for structurally complex therapeutic modalities including bispecific antibodies, antibody-drug conjugates, fusion proteins, incretins, and radioligand therapeutics. The work addresses an

item.title: A patient-centered conceptual model and measurement framework for migraine experience developed by the Migraine Clinical Outcome Assessment System
item.description: Researchers developed a comprehensive, patient-centered conceptual model and measurement framework for assessing migraine experience through the Migraine Clinical Outcome Assessment System. The framework is evidence-based and designed to standardize how migraine impact is evaluated from the patient

item.title: Google’s nobel-winning AI expert departing for anthropic
item.description: John Jumper, a Nobel Prize-winning Google DeepMind artificial intelligence scientist, is leaving the company for Anthropic. “After nearly 9 years, I have decided to leave Google DeepMind and join Anthropic,” Jumper posted on the social media platform X Friday…

item.title: AI under trump's control: can france still avoid digital dependence?
item.description: Article URL: https://thenewassociationwebmasters.blogspot.com/2026/06/ai-under-american-control-can-france.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48619005 Points: 7 # Comments: 1

item.title: 'outdated' red tape holding back AI cancer breakthrough, scientists say
item.description: Rosie the dog had her tumours treated by an mRNA vaccine developed with the help of artificial intelligence. Her owner had decided to explore treatment options using AI chatbots.

item.title: AI scribes promise relief for clinicians yet canada’s experience shows the trade-offs are real
item.description: AI transcription represents both opportunity and caution. Used properly, it can reduce clinician burden, improve efficiency, and enhance patient interaction. Used poorly, it risks introducing subtle but significant errors into a system where precision matters…

item.title: How to leverage sms marketing agencies to increase return visits | technical guides
item.description: This technical guide explains how IT and venue operations teams can integrate SMS marketing agencies with Purple's Guest WiFi platform to capture verified…

item.title: Senator sounds alarm over g7 'nightmare scene' exposing 'state-like power' of corporations
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy says a single image from this week's G7 summit captures one of his deepest fears about the growing power of the tech industry: the chief executives of major artificial intelligence companies seated at the table alongside presidents and prime…

item.title: Anthropic's mythos mess just keeps getting more complicated
item.description: It sure seems a lot like the Trump administration is just bulling Anthropic for not acquiescing to its every move, and its the cybersecurity community who'll suffer for it

item.title: When the trump administration cracks down on anthropic, who benefits? | techcrunch
item.description: On the new episode of Equity, we discussed what actually prompted the administration's latest moves against Anthropic, and what this might mean for the AI ecosystem.

item.title: CHIP-AML22: a complex clinical trial in de novo pediatric AML patients, including a gemtuzumab ozogamicin randomization and targeted therapy with quizartinib in eligible subgroups, within the NOPHO-DB-SHIP consortium
item.description: CHIP-AML22 is a complex clinical trial evaluating treatment strategies in newly diagnosed pediatric acute myeloid leukemia (AML) patients through a multi-center consortium. The trial includes a randomized assessment of gemtuzumab ozogamicin and targeted therapy with quizartinib for eligible patient

item.title: Alphafold pioneer john jumper joins anthropic
item.description: New Delhi : Renowned scientist John Jumper announced his exit from Google DeepMind after nearly nine years to join Anthropic following a short break. Jumper shared the update on social media platform X on Friday, expressing gratitude for his long ass

item.title: Alphafold pioneer john jumper joins anthropic after deepmind exit
item.description: Renowned scientist John Jumper announced his exit from Google DeepMind after nearly nine years to join Anthropic following a short break. Jumper shared the update on social media platform X on Friday , expressing gratitude for his long association with DeepMi…

item.title: Nobel winner john jumper to leave google deepmind for anthropic
item.description: Jumper has been a key member of Google’s AI coding development team and his departure furthers strains the tech giant’s efforts to beat Anthropic, OpenAI and Elon Musk’s SpaceX in the race to build the most powerful AI models.

item.title: 'it’s no longer enough for an app to tell you what to do. people want to know why': fitness app fitbod's founder on the reason behind the AI fitness boom
item.description: Allen Chen talks all things Fitbod, including what’s coming in the coming months.

item.title: Claude fable 5 on bedrock requires sharing inference data with anthropic
item.description: Using Claude Fable 5 or Mythos 5 on Amazon Bedrock requires opting into provider_data_share, sending prompts and outputs to Anthropic for 30-day retention with human review. Previous Bedrock models kept inference data inside the AWS boundary. Three days after…

item.title: Should everyone be on glp-1s? what the science really tells us about cancer prevention and other benefits
item.description: The laundry list of possible health benefits from GLP-1 drugs seems to get longer by the day, from cancer prevention to combating addiction. But while there’s no question these medications show immense promise beyond their use for diabetes and weight loss, sc…

item.title: Hackers claim to leak stolen madison square garden data
item.description: Plus: Gay bars in San Francisco using face scanners, France quits Palantir, Apple plans to change its private email and more.

item.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
item.description: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

item.title: US scientist john jumper to leave google deepmind for anthropic
item.description: Senior research scientist John Jumper said on Friday he would leave Google DeepMind to join AI startup Anthropic, the latest ‌high-profile departure at the Big Tech giant's AI research and development division. Jumper, ‌who won a Nobel prize alongside Google'…

item.title: AI may actually be making healthcare more expensive
item.description: Artificial intelligence is poised to cause commercial medical care costs to surge over the next year.

item.title: AI risk—beyond replacement, toward responsibility
item.description: AI risk isn’t just about regulation or replacement. It’s about where uncertainty belongs and who absorbs the consequences when systems don’t work as planned.

item.title: Robots in the orchard: how automation is reshaping greenhouse horticulture in canada
item.description: Advances are being made in orchard robotics, where systems are being designed to locate fruit across multi-dimensional canopies. The post Robots in the orchard: How automation is reshaping greenhouse horticulture in Canada appeared first on Digital Journal.

item.title: Agentic ai’s challenge is getting agents to act like a team, not a crowd
item.description: Adding more artificial intelligence agents to the workflow doesn’t make an enterprise smarter. In fact, it can make operations harder to manage. The problem is not the capabilities of individual agents but how well they work together. Many enterprises are mov…
